sys_hub_flow.description: Parent Flow Designer flow that orchestrates every status transition for cases of type Complaint on x_casemgmt_case. OnUpdate trigger filtered on type=Complaint AND status change. Dispatches to validate_open_transition, validate_inprogress_transition, validate_pending_transition, validate_resolved_transition, or validate_closed_transition subflow under ./sub_flows/ based on the new status value via a 5-branch If/Else-If ladder. Replaces the ArkCase ChangeCaseFileStateService + ChangeCaseStatusWorkflowListener + CaseFileQueueHandler stack (the BPMN/Activiti engine is eliminated entirely per AAP Section 0.1.2). Runs as user_who_triggers so that the role-based gate in validate_closed_transition (gs.getUser().hasRole('x_casemgmt_case_manager')) evaluates against the saving user rather than the system user - non-negotiable per AAP Section 0.7.4. All transition rules and verbatim error messages are sourced from AAP Sections 0.5.5 and 0.7.4 and surfaced from the dispatched subflows or supporting business rules (../business_rules/x_casemgmt_block_draft_backtransition.xml and ../business_rules/x_casemgmt_block_terminal_closed.xml). NO hardcoded sys_ids in any reference field - the trigger's table reference, every subflow call, and every scope reference resolve by name (table=x_casemgmt_case, subflow_name=x_casemgmt_validate_*_transition, scope=x_casemgmt). Active=true and status=published per AAP Section 0.7.3 Gate 7 so Update Set commit immediately enables the state machine. Functionally identical to ./general_inquiry_state_machine.xml except the trigger filter is type=Complaint rather than type=General Inquiry - the duplication is intentional per AAP Section 0.4.3 to keep per-type trigger filtering explicit and to allow future per-type customization. Contains zero gs.eventQueue() calls per the email-disabled constraint and zero hardcoded sys_id reference literals.
sys_hub_flow.latest_snapshot: { "name": "x_casemgmt_complaint_state_machine", "label": "Complaint State Machine", "type": "flow", "description": "Parent state-machine flow for cases of type=Complaint. OnUpdate trigger filtered on type=Complaint AND status change; dispatches to one of five transition-validation subflows based on the new status value. Replaces ArkCase ChangeCaseFileStateService + ChangeCaseStatusWorkflowListener + CaseFileQueueHandler. Runs as user_who_triggers for correct role-check evaluation. Functionally identical to general_inquiry_state_machine.xml except the trigger filter is type=Complaint rather than type=General Inquiry.", "scope": "x_casemgmt", "run_as": "user_who_triggers", "category": "flow", "trigger": { "id": "trigger_record_updated", "name": "On Complaint case status change", "type": "record", "operation": "updated", "trigger_type": "record_updated", "table": "x_casemgmt_case", "reference_table": "x_casemgmt_case", "condition": "type=Complaint^EQ", "condition_query": "type=Complaint", "filter_status_changes_only": true, "filter_field_changed": "status", "filter_field_changes_to_any": true, "run_trigger": "always", "description": "Fires on every update to a row in x_casemgmt_case where (a) the row's type field equals the literal string 'Complaint' (Title Case, single word; this is the choice LABEL value declared in ../choices/sys_choice_case_type.xml sequence 200, NOT a sys_id) AND (b) the row's status field changed value as part of the update. The trigger does NOT fire on inserts, on row deletions, or on updates that leave status unchanged. The 'condition' string uses ServiceNow's GlideRecord encoded-query syntax: type=Complaint^EQ means 'type field exactly equals Complaint' (the ^EQ suffix is the equality operator marker - some platform releases serialize it as '=' alone, others append ^EQ). The filter_status_changes_only flag is the platform-canonical way to express 'fire only when the status field changes value' which is more selective than firing on every update." }, "inputs": [], "outputs": [], "stages": [], "actions": [ { "id": "step_if_status_open", "order": 100, "name": "If status is Open", "type": "if", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'Open' (Title Case, single word). On match, dispatches to the validate_open_transition subflow which validates that assigned_group is populated. The condition uses the new value of the field (current.status), not the previous value (previous.status), because the trigger has already established that status changed and we are interested in the destination state.", "condition": "current.status == 'Open'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_open", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_open_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_open_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_open_transition subflow which checks that assigned_group is populated before allowing Draft -> Open. The subflow uses x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.canTransitionToOpen and throws a blocking form-level error if the precondition fails. Subflow lookup is by NAME (not by sys_id) per AAP Section 0.7.2 No-Hardcoded-Sys_ID constraint.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] }, { "id": "step_elseif_status_inprogress", "order": 200, "name": "Else if status is In Progress", "type": "elseif", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'In Progress' (Title Case, two words separated by single ASCII space). On match, dispatches to the validate_inprogress_transition subflow which validates that assigned_agent is populated AND is a member of assigned_group, and clears pending_reason if the source state was Pending.", "condition": "current.status == 'In Progress'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_inprogress", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_inprogress_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_inprogress_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_inprogress_transition subflow which checks that assigned_agent is populated AND is a member of assigned_group before allowing Open -> In Progress (or Pending -> In Progress). The subflow uses x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.canTransitionToInProgress and throws a blocking form-level error if the compound precondition fails. Subflow lookup is by NAME per AAP Section 0.7.2.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] }, { "id": "step_elseif_status_pending", "order": 300, "name": "Else if status is Pending", "type": "elseif", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'Pending'. On match, dispatches to the validate_pending_transition subflow which has no precondition gate but DOES set the pending_reason field (Awaiting Info / Awaiting Third Party / Other) per AAP Section 0.5.5.", "condition": "current.status == 'Pending'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_pending", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_pending_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_pending_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_pending_transition subflow which stamps pending_reason on the case row. No precondition gate; the In Progress -> Pending transition is unconditionally allowed per AAP Section 0.5.5. The pending_reason value is captured from the calling user's prompt input and persisted via Update Record action inside the subflow. Subflow lookup is by NAME per AAP Section 0.7.2.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] }, { "id": "step_elseif_status_resolved", "order": 400, "name": "Else if status is Resolved", "type": "elseif", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'Resolved'. On match, dispatches to the validate_resolved_transition subflow which queries child x_casemgmt_case_task records via GlideAggregate and surfaces the VERBATIM AAP Section 0.7.4 error message 'All tasks must be closed before resolving this case.' if any open child tasks exist.", "condition": "current.status == 'Resolved'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_resolved", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_resolved_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_resolved_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_resolved_transition subflow which enforces the all-tasks-closed gate before allowing In Progress -> Resolved. The subflow uses x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.canTransitionToResolved (which delegates to GlideAggregate('x_casemgmt_case_task') with addQuery('case', caseSysId) and addQuery('status', '!=', 'Closed')). Throws the VERBATIM AAP Section 0.7.4 error message on failure. Replaces ArkCase CaseFileTasksService (which used Solr search; Solr is out of POC scope per AAP Section 0.3.2). Subflow lookup is by NAME per AAP Section 0.7.2.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] }, { "id": "step_elseif_status_closed", "order": 500, "name": "Else if status is Closed", "type": "elseif", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'Closed'. On match, dispatches to the validate_closed_transition subflow which enforces the case_manager role gate before allowing Resolved -> Closed and auto-sets the closed_date field.", "condition": "current.status == 'Closed'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_closed", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_closed_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_closed_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_closed_transition subflow which (a) checks gs.getUser().hasRole('x_casemgmt_case_manager') and (b) auto-sets closed_date = gs.nowDateTime() per AAP Section 0.5.5. The role check evaluates against the saving user because this parent flow's run_as is user_who_triggers (NOT system_user, which would bypass the role gate). Subflow lookup is by NAME and the role lookup inside the subflow is also by NAME (x_casemgmt_case_manager) per AAP Section 0.7.2.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] } ], "tables_referenced": [ "x_casemgmt_case", "x_casemgmt_case_task" ], "subflows_called": [ "x_casemgmt_validate_open_transition", "x_casemgmt_validate_inprogress_transition", "x_casemgmt_validate_pending_transition", "x_casemgmt_validate_resolved_transition", "x_casemgmt_validate_closed_transition" ], "script_includes_used": [ "x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator" ] }
sys_hub_flow.master_snapshot: { "name": "x_casemgmt_complaint_state_machine", "label": "Complaint State Machine", "type": "flow", "description": "Parent state-machine flow for cases of type=Complaint. OnUpdate trigger filtered on type=Complaint AND status change; dispatches to one of five transition-validation subflows based on the new status value. Replaces ArkCase ChangeCaseFileStateService + ChangeCaseStatusWorkflowListener + CaseFileQueueHandler. Runs as user_who_triggers for correct role-check evaluation. Functionally identical to general_inquiry_state_machine.xml except the trigger filter is type=Complaint rather than type=General Inquiry.", "scope": "x_casemgmt", "run_as": "user_who_triggers", "category": "flow", "trigger": { "id": "trigger_record_updated", "name": "On Complaint case status change", "type": "record", "operation": "updated", "trigger_type": "record_updated", "table": "x_casemgmt_case", "reference_table": "x_casemgmt_case", "condition": "type=Complaint^EQ", "condition_query": "type=Complaint", "filter_status_changes_only": true, "filter_field_changed": "status", "filter_field_changes_to_any": true, "run_trigger": "always", "description": "Fires on every update to a row in x_casemgmt_case where (a) the row's type field equals the literal string 'Complaint' (Title Case, single word; this is the choice LABEL value declared in ../choices/sys_choice_case_type.xml sequence 200, NOT a sys_id) AND (b) the row's status field changed value as part of the update. The trigger does NOT fire on inserts, on row deletions, or on updates that leave status unchanged. The 'condition' string uses ServiceNow's GlideRecord encoded-query syntax: type=Complaint^EQ means 'type field exactly equals Complaint' (the ^EQ suffix is the equality operator marker - some platform releases serialize it as '=' alone, others append ^EQ). The filter_status_changes_only flag is the platform-canonical way to express 'fire only when the status field changes value' which is more selective than firing on every update." }, "inputs": [], "outputs": [], "stages": [], "actions": [ { "id": "step_if_status_open", "order": 100, "name": "If status is Open", "type": "if", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'Open' (Title Case, single word). On match, dispatches to the validate_open_transition subflow which validates that assigned_group is populated. The condition uses the new value of the field (current.status), not the previous value (previous.status), because the trigger has already established that status changed and we are interested in the destination state.", "condition": "current.status == 'Open'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_open", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_open_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_open_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_open_transition subflow which checks that assigned_group is populated before allowing Draft -> Open. The subflow uses x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.canTransitionToOpen and throws a blocking form-level error if the precondition fails. Subflow lookup is by NAME (not by sys_id) per AAP Section 0.7.2 No-Hardcoded-Sys_ID constraint.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] }, { "id": "step_elseif_status_inprogress", "order": 200, "name": "Else if status is In Progress", "type": "elseif", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'In Progress' (Title Case, two words separated by single ASCII space). On match, dispatches to the validate_inprogress_transition subflow which validates that assigned_agent is populated AND is a member of assigned_group, and clears pending_reason if the source state was Pending.", "condition": "current.status == 'In Progress'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_inprogress", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_inprogress_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_inprogress_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_inprogress_transition subflow which checks that assigned_agent is populated AND is a member of assigned_group before allowing Open -> In Progress (or Pending -> In Progress). The subflow uses x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.canTransitionToInProgress and throws a blocking form-level error if the compound precondition fails. Subflow lookup is by NAME per AAP Section 0.7.2.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] }, { "id": "step_elseif_status_pending", "order": 300, "name": "Else if status is Pending", "type": "elseif", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'Pending'. On match, dispatches to the validate_pending_transition subflow which has no precondition gate but DOES set the pending_reason field (Awaiting Info / Awaiting Third Party / Other) per AAP Section 0.5.5.", "condition": "current.status == 'Pending'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_pending", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_pending_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_pending_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_pending_transition subflow which stamps pending_reason on the case row. No precondition gate; the In Progress -> Pending transition is unconditionally allowed per AAP Section 0.5.5. The pending_reason value is captured from the calling user's prompt input and persisted via Update Record action inside the subflow. Subflow lookup is by NAME per AAP Section 0.7.2.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] }, { "id": "step_elseif_status_resolved", "order": 400, "name": "Else if status is Resolved", "type": "elseif", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'Resolved'. On match, dispatches to the validate_resolved_transition subflow which queries child x_casemgmt_case_task records via GlideAggregate and surfaces the VERBATIM AAP Section 0.7.4 error message 'All tasks must be closed before resolving this case.' if any open child tasks exist.", "condition": "current.status == 'Resolved'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_resolved", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_resolved_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_resolved_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_resolved_transition subflow which enforces the all-tasks-closed gate before allowing In Progress -> Resolved. The subflow uses x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator.canTransitionToResolved (which delegates to GlideAggregate('x_casemgmt_case_task') with addQuery('case', caseSysId) and addQuery('status', '!=', 'Closed')). Throws the VERBATIM AAP Section 0.7.4 error message on failure. Replaces ArkCase CaseFileTasksService (which used Solr search; Solr is out of POC scope per AAP Section 0.3.2). Subflow lookup is by NAME per AAP Section 0.7.2.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] }, { "id": "step_elseif_status_closed", "order": 500, "name": "Else if status is Closed", "type": "elseif", "description": "Branches when the case's new status value is exactly 'Closed'. On match, dispatches to the validate_closed_transition subflow which enforces the case_manager role gate before allowing Resolved -> Closed and auto-sets the closed_date field.", "condition": "current.status == 'Closed'", "actions": [ { "id": "step_call_validate_closed", "order": 100, "name": "Call x_casemgmt_validate_closed_transition", "type": "call_subflow", "subflow_name": "x_casemgmt_validate_closed_transition", "subflow_scope": "x_casemgmt", "description": "Invokes the validate_closed_transition subflow which (a) checks gs.getUser().hasRole('x_casemgmt_case_manager') and (b) auto-sets closed_date = gs.nowDateTime() per AAP Section 0.5.5. The role check evaluates against the saving user because this parent flow's run_as is user_who_triggers (NOT system_user, which would bypass the role gate). Subflow lookup is by NAME and the role lookup inside the subflow is also by NAME (x_casemgmt_case_manager) per AAP Section 0.7.2.", "inputs": { "case_record": "${trigger.current}" } } ] } ], "tables_referenced": [ "x_casemgmt_case", "x_casemgmt_case_task" ], "subflows_called": [ "x_casemgmt_validate_open_transition", "x_casemgmt_validate_inprogress_transition", "x_casemgmt_validate_pending_transition", "x_casemgmt_validate_resolved_transition", "x_casemgmt_validate_closed_transition" ], "script_includes_used": [ "x_casemgmt.CaseTransitionValidator" ] }
sys_hub_flow.name: x_casemgmt_complaint_state_machine
